description: The initial Clarity platform: AI receptionist with secure login and accounts, a master admin portal for managing client businesses, contact records with custom fields, saved filters, and CSV export.
Added the automations system (automatic email/text follow-ups via triggers, conditions, and actions) and hardened security (super-admin role, rate limiting, authenticated webhooks, secure cookies).
Contacts: multi-select, bulk actions, soft-delete with a 30-day Recycle Bin, and customizable columns; added visual themes.
Added the Learning Center (in-app guides), rebranding, more contact operations, and contact identity rules (email/phone requirements).
Added inbound webhook intake (capture leads from external sources) and a scheduled-jobs engine for time-based automations.
Added automation presets, a branching setup wizard, and an expanded library of automation templates.
Added automation pair-linking (connect related automations).
Added a toolbar to the automations list (search, filter, and manage).
Learning Center: removed placeholder screenshots and expanded the Automations section into nine guides.
Added the Records backbone — the data foundation for record types like Jobs and Bookings.
Records: fields can be defined per record type (different fields for Jobs vs Bookings, etc.).
Added the Jobs page — create and manage Jobs, with links between Jobs and contacts.
Jobs: brought to parity with contacts, including import and sample data.
Fixed and refined linking contacts to Jobs — search, visibility, and showing email/phone in the picker.
Fields can be grouped into sections with a two-column layout on record pages.
Drag fields into sections, with spacing improvements and link-a-Job refinements.
Record pages: added column-width controls and pipeline stages.
Fixed a visual flicker (blink) on record pages.
Added Job types (subtypes), each with its own pipeline of stages.
Added a kanban board on the Job page to drag linked contacts (candidates) through pipeline stages.
Automations can trigger when a contact's pipeline stage changes.
Automations can act on Jobs and records, not just contacts.
Automations can fan out to every contact linked to a Job, with a send gate to control recipients.
Pipeline stage changes are now tracked as history, recorded on every move, with past moves backfilled.
Added a stalled-candidate nudge — automatically follow up when a candidate sits in a stage too long.
Added loop-safety so automations can't trigger each other endlessly (depth limit plus actor tracking).
Automations can write to a record's stage or fields as an action.
Automations can use record and Job fields as conditions.
Automation builder: actions grouped with descriptions, plus a live preview of what an automation will do.
Added more automation templates and wizard options.
Relabeled automation actions to describe honestly what they do.
Automations can perform real actions on records (create and update Jobs and fields).
Redesigned the Settings area with a new shell and layout.
Added per-business relabeling: rename app terms (Contacts, Jobs, Stages, and more) from a Labels editor in Settings, with automatic pluralization.
Applied per-business labels across all pages, the automation builder, and the Learning Center.
Themes can now be set per business.
Reports: added record-level reporting (report on Jobs and records, not just contacts).
Added a pipeline service that backs record reporting.
Added a status editor to manage pipeline statuses and stages for record types.
UI cleanup across compose, records, reports, and theming.
Added a styled confirmation dialog, replacing the plain browser pop-up.
Added a swimlane board view for contacts across pipeline stages.
Added the Home dashboard.
Reports: added an additional reporting view and controls.
Added per-business navigation customization: rename, reorder, and hide sidebar items.
Added impersonation — super-admins can securely act as another user (view-only, role-downgraded, with a master-hub lockout and a start/exit control).
Hardened tenant scoping so data never leaks across businesses (fail-safe).
The automation send-email action now uses the rich-text editor with subject lines and templates.
Added portal invitations — invite users to a business via an email link instead of temporary passwords.
Unified the new-portal setup into a single guided screen (removed the per-card Set up button and aligned the toolbar).
Fixed a startup crash caused by duplicate record-type seeding, and added a process-level error guard.
Moved the Simulate call button to the Calls toolbar so it's always visible.
Improved call simulation with harder multi-turn scenarios to stress-test the AI.
Made the AI receptionist warmer and more natural, with better info collection and graceful handling of unknowns.
Added per-business AI Instructions (custom guidance for the receptionist), Calls list pagination, and an edit audit trail.
Fixed AI Instructions not pre-filling when the page loads.
Tuned the AI prompt so owner instructions take priority and calls close out properly on wrap-up.
Four fixes: calls finalize correctly on hang-up, Calls footer paging, an AI honesty rule, and AI Instructions panel alignment.
Added a Twilio status-callback helper needed for reliable call finalization.
Added a per-business on/off toggle for the AI receptionist.
Three UI fixes: navigation refresh, the nav editor's Calls row, and Calls table rows.
Added an OWNER role (top tier above Super Admin), recognized across the back-end and front-end.
Added an AUDITOR role and 3-day account expiry for temporary access.
Added a per-business Users button and fixed the Remove-link action.
Restricted master user creation to Super Admin and Auditor, and fixed the dialog opening in the wrong mode.
User creation now goes through email invitations, with no manual passwords.
Added a configurable sender email, inline name editing, and carrying the invitee's name through the invite.
Fixed sender-card spacing and the sidebar name updating on self-rename.
Added the premium voice path (Twilio ConversationRelay plus ElevenLabs) for natural-sounding calls.
Premium voice: added full inbound-message logging and fixed partial-prompt handling.
The Calls page now auto-refreshes live for in-progress calls.
Added a per-business voice mode (Off, Standard, or Premium) controlling how calls are answered.
Premium voice: added keepalive and better drop logging; the Calls page refreshes when you return to the tab.
More reliable call finalization — sweep stale calls, finalize on hang-up and close, show a live reason, and stop calls reverting to In progress.
Added a premium voice picker so each business can choose the AI's voice.
Outbound email now sends from a verified domain (noreply@vaala.io).
The per-business Team tab now uses the shared email-invite flow, with no temporary passwords.
Learning Center: added guides for Calls, Jobs, and all Settings, and removed master-only content.
Businesses can upload their own logo (white-label) in Appearance; added a permanent 'A Vaala product' tag.
Added Feedback ticketing — users submit tickets per business, and the master hub manages them.
New default Clarity branding and logo, rendered inline so it always shows in the header and on login; added a demo-portal seed script.
The master Users page now shows only your operator team, not business-scoped users.
Auditor invitation emails now include a Quick-Reference Guide PDF.
Show pending invitees in user lists, fixed Feedback margins, and audited the Learning Center.
The Feedback page now fills the full content width.
Time-based automations now run automatically every couple of minutes.
Added a contact 'source' field and a 'New call lead' automation trigger.
Added a 'Notify the business' automation action — email or text the owner, not the lead.
Automations cleanup: renamed 'Intent' to 'Reason', removed filler templates, and added call-lead and notify-business templates.
Added the Bookings foundation — a Booking record type with a real appointment date and time.
Fixed booking times to store and show exactly as entered (wall-clock), avoiding timezone shifts.
The AI receptionist now captures appointment date/time and service into a Booking during calls.
Added the availability engine that computes open booking slots from business hours, with a read-only slots preview.
Added a scheduling editor to set business hours (including split shifts), per-service durations, and buffers.
Added a read-only week/day Bookings calendar with open-hours shading.
Bookings calendar polish and fixes: colorblind-safe status colors, a legend, a now-line, closed-day labels, and nav highlighting.
Bookings calendar: click a slot to create a prefilled booking or click a booking to edit, with a double-booking advisory lock.
Added a per-business double-booking toggle — allow overlaps, or warn manual users and hard-block the AI.
Added Resources (staff and rooms) — manage and label them, and assign bookings to a resource.
The Bookings calendar is now resource-aware, with per-resource columns and a per-resource double-booking lock.
Each resource can have its own working hours, falling back to business hours.
Fixes for the advisory lock, closed-day warn-with-override, the Closed header layout, and unassigned bookings.
Added per-resource appointment durations and buffers across slot-finding, overlap checks, and calendar sizing.
Surface the assigned resource as a Bookings list column and a small dot on calendar blocks.
Calendar fixes: removed a 'Today' flash, smoother re-renders, and a two-step click-to-create.
Bookings now drive automations — Booking Created and Status Changed triggers, plus confirmation and no-show templates.
Added hour-precise appointment reminders that send automatically before bookings.
Appointment details now fill in correctly in confirmation and no-show messages.
The AI receptionist now captures and honors the requested staff member when booking.
Added a read-only availability check the AI can use to see open slots.
The AI receptionist checks real availability mid-call and says 'let me check' while looking up.
The AI receptionist now knows and can state business and staff hours.
The AI now offers actual bookable slots (not just open hours) and asks which staff member.
The AI no longer ends the call at booking confirmation — it captures the caller's name and number first.
The AI only asks which staff member when there's more than one, and stops repeating 'how can I assist.'
The AI offers specific start times instead of ranges and handles noon/12 PM clearly.
The AI keeps the conversation on track after a caller interrupts (barge-in).
The AI gives honest availability reasons (booked vs closed) and stops inventing 'booked' or picking staff itself.
Added a verified Caller ID field (separate from the editable phone), shown in Calls; bookings follow the spoken number.
The AI summarizes availability into natural ranges instead of listing every slot.
Contacts table: show all chosen columns with a sticky header and horizontal scroll (50 rows per page), with image thumbnails.
Owners and Super Admins can delete resolved feedback tickets in both views.
Added feedback ticket CSV export (reusing the contacts export dialog), one row per reply, with sensible default columns.
Added all-portals feedback export from the master hub — export one or all businesses, with history.
Restricted feedback export to Owner, Super Admin, and Auditor, and made it impersonation-safe.
Feedback tickets now support attachment links (added on create or later), with URL validation and automatic https.
Feedback export now includes numbered Attachment columns.
Added a per-business timezone setting (a picker in Settings) so times display correctly.
Added encrypted storage for Google Calendar connections and resource-to-calendar mappings.
Added Google Calendar connect and disconnect (read-only OAuth) with token refresh and a connect screen.
Added Google calendar listing and resource-to-calendar mapping, with a mapping screen.
Added timezone conversion at the Google Calendar boundary.
Bookings now honor an explicit end time across availability, overlap checks, and calendar sizing.
Google-synced bookings are read-only in the app — only the sync can change them.
Added Google-to-Clarity sync that pulls external bookings in (read-only, gated off by default).
Added a read-only Google sync preview showing mappings, the window, and raw events.
Request Google write access and detect when it's granted; expose sync health.
Show Google-synced bookings as read-only with a badge, plus a sync-health card.
Added Google write-out push to mirror Clarity bookings to Google (create, update, delete), gated off by default.
Google push: richer event descriptions, accurate durations, and cleanup when bookings move between calendars.
The AI books cautiously when Google sync is stale or degraded.
Added a two-way Google sync on/off toggle that auto-enables when you connect and map a calendar.
Fixed business-wide availability by combining each resource's openings (Unassigned no longer blocks named staff), and auto-assign a free resource when needed.
The AI books with a free named staff member — it books if one is free and asks if several are.
The AI records the staff member it announces and captures the spoken callback number.
A single call can no longer create duplicate bookings (deduplicated by contact and time).
The AI records every booking detail it announces and confirms before announcing.
Bookings are no longer silently lost — rescue a named-but-unavailable staff member and log every failure.
Booking-capture overhaul — the backend owns the final booking commit, carries the chosen staff forward, and never loses a booking.
Premium voice: if no caller audio arrives, the AI re-prompts and then ends the call gracefully.
Added an Integrations area (Twilio, OpenAI, and Google cards with role-based editing) under Settings, with brand logos; moved the timezone setting to Bookings.
Bookings calendar header redesign — an inline timezone, a 'Go to Today' button, a 2x2 legend, and a Calendar Sync block.
Recycle Bin foundation — record who deleted each contact and record.
The Recycle Bin now covers records too, with per-type tables to view, restore, or permanently remove deleted items.
Recycle Bin: a read-only preview of a deleted item that mirrors its real Contact, Job, or Booking page, showing who deleted it and when, with Restore.
Added the Change Log — a master-hub page listing every change shipped to the app.
